flashcards.back: A task in OpenMP is a unit of work defined using #pragma omp task, executed asynchronously by any thread. Used for irregular parallelism like recursive algorithms.
Synchronization directive that pauses current task until all child tasks created in its scope complete. Ensures task dependencies are respected.
Use depend clause: #pragma omp task depend(in:x) for reads, depend(out:y) for writes. Controls execution order based on data dependencies.
Load-balancing technique where idle threads steal tasks from other threads' queues. Enables efficient dynamic scheduling in OpenMP.

flashcards.front: When to use tasks vs parallel for?
flashcards.back: Use tasks for irregular workloads (tree traversals, graphs). Use parallel for for regular data-parallel loops with predictable iterations.
1. Recursive algorithms (Fibonacci) 2. Unstructured grids 3. Producer-consumer patterns 4. Divide-and-conquer methods like quicksort
taskgroup waits for all descendant tasks (including nested ones). taskwait only waits for immediate child tasks.

flashcards.front: Why use #pragma omp single with tasks?
flashcards.back: Ensures only one thread creates initial tasks to avoid redundant work. Typically used inside parallel region for task generation.

flashcards.front: What causes false sharing in tasks?
flashcards.back: When multiple tasks modify different variables in same cache line. Causes unnecessary cache invalidations and performance degradation.
1. Align data structures to cache boundaries 2. Use padding between variables 3. Make frequently accessed variables thread-private

flashcards.front: Syntax for task dependency on array element
flashcards.back: #pragma omp task depend(in: arr[5]). OpenMP 4.5+ supports array section dependencies using arr[lower:length] syntax.
Variables are shared by default except loop counters. Use private clause or declare variables inside task block for privatization.
Use #pragma omp taskgroup with reduction clause. Each task updates local copy, combined at taskgroup exit. (OpenMP 5.0+ supports task reductions)
Efficiently handles load imbalance by assigning tasks to idle threads at runtime. Ideal for unpredictable workloads.
Task creation and scheduling costs. Mitigate by creating coarse-grained tasks or using taskloop for loop parallelism.
Allows current task to be suspended so thread can execute other tasks. Useful for task cancellation or priority management.
Fibonacci sequence: Each task spawns two child tasks for fib(n-1) and fib(n-2), using taskwait to combine results.
Combines task and loop parallelism. Divides loop iterations into tasks, automatically load-balanced across threads.
1. taskwait: Wait for child tasks 2. taskgroup: Wait for all descendants 3. Dependencies: Control execution order via depend clauses
Tasks modifying shared data must follow cache coherence protocols (MESI). False sharing degrades performance by causing cache line transfers.
#pragma omp [parallel] [single] { #pragma omp task [clauses] { ... } }. Typically used within parallel region with single master task creator.
Sections are static (fixed at compile time), tasks are dynamic. Tasks support dependencies and recursive creation, sections do not.
OpenMP doesn't support native task priorities. Workaround: Order tasks using dependencies or manual priority queues.
Uses shared memory model. Tasks can access variables from enclosing scope unless made private via data-sharing clauses.
Producers create tasks, consumers execute them. Implement using task dependencies or #pragma omp task untied for task migration.
